A.H.Wheeler

# A.H.Wheeler

### The Indian Railways bookshop

[](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

![Ratnakar Sadasyula's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R73I!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F81a2c542-8b3f-4ade-98d2-a40950cffbe3_2640x1980.jpeg)

[Ratnakar Sadasyula](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

Dec 03, 2020

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

2

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

1

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Share

We might have come across [A.H.Wheeler,](https://www.quora.com/Why-are-all-bookstores-on-Indian-Railway-stations-named-A-H-Wheeler) many a time, while travelling by train in India. And passed time on long journeys with the books we bought from there. However it’s founder was actually a Frenchman, Emilie Moreau.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!3tWM!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F25ca6e88-ca01-459a-846c-0f90af42b0fc_300x168.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!3tWM!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F25ca6e88-ca01-459a-846c-0f90af42b0fc_300x168.jpeg)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!C6po!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ffcfc956e-2ef3-4cbe-a803-482c2b2395bb_260x194.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!C6po!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ffcfc956e-2ef3-4cbe-a803-482c2b2395bb_260x194.jpeg)

Moreau was a representative of Bird and Company , an English firm at Allahabad in 1857 and had a passion for books. He wanted to leave Allahabad, however he had a long pending issue, to dispose off his entire collection of books, journals and magazines. One day at Allahabad Railway Station, he saw that passengers were keen on reading magazines or journals, for passing away the time. This is when he hit on the idea of setting up a book store, seeing the potential. He named it after Arthur Henry Wheeler, a close friend of his and also one of the leading book sellers in London then, he felt a English name would have more brand value. And thus A.H.Wheeler started from Allahabad in 1877.

With the expansion of the Railways, the bookstores too started spreading to all stations, and soon A.H.Wheeler's was a common sight on all railway platforms, attracting the passengers as well as those with a love for reading. Initially it only catered to the English speaking population, most of the employees too were Englishmen or Anglo Indians. However when T.K.Banerjee joined the Kolkata office ij 1899, there was a significant change. TKB, earned praise with his efficiency in handling the accounts and auditing, and was sent to the Head Office in Allahabad. Under TKB, the Wheeler book stalls changed too, now they began to advertise for companies associated with Indian Railways, but the biggest step was selling books in regional languages too. During the freedom movement, A.H.Wheeler's in a way played it's part too, by publishing various news items related to the movement. Impresed with TKB's work and diligence, Moreau stood by his side, throughout and when he left for England in 1937, he transferred the ownership to him.

It was the freedom movement that made A.H.Wheeler quite popular, when it began to sell journals, magazines, newspapers dedicated to the nationalist cause. People came to know of the Dandi March, trial of Bhagat Singh through such newspapers. That apart it also sold books, written by Gandhi, Nehru, as well as thinkers like Aurobindo, Tagore, Dr.Radhakrishnan, the works of poets like Tulsidas, and books written by Sarat Chandra, Prem Chand, Mulkraj Anand. For many leaders of the nationalist movement, and political leaders, A.H.Wheeler became one of their companions, through it's books and periodicals. So much so after India got independence, it became an integral part of the railways.

Source: My answer on [Quora](https://www.quora.com/Why-are-all-bookstores-on-Indian-Railway-stations-named-A-H-Wheeler/answer/Ratnakar-Sadasyula-%E0%B0%B0%E0%B0%A4%E0%B1%8D%E0%B0%A8%E0%B0%BE%E0%B0%95%E0%B0%B0%E0%B1%8D-%E0%B0%B8%E0%B0%A6%E0%B0%B8%E0%B1%8D%E0%B0%AF%E0%B1%81%E0%B0%B2) here
